initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is proposing the exact same trade I proposed. This is a good sign! I will accept this trade as it covers my costs and provides a small profit. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Excellent! I accept the trade. 1 X for 45 ZUP sounds perfect. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and in the correct order, with valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting the trade at 45 ZUP is good as my cost is 40 ZUP. Any less would reduce my profit, and 45 is a reasonable first-trade acceptance.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent already proposed the same trade, indicating high likelihood of acceptance.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> clearly explains why I'm accepting the trade, and the <message> reinforces that acceptance. There are no contradictions.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have only used 2 proposals and am now accepting, adhering to the rule limits.
</critique>
